title: digitalis × coutinhoi
intro: Digitalis × coutinhoi is a sterile interspecific hybrid of the foxglove plants Digitalis purpurea and Digitalis ferruginea, exhibiting characteristics of both parent species. The plant contains a range of bioactive compounds, including glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The primary medicinal properties of Digitalis × coutinhoi include cardiotonic and diuretic effects, making it useful for treating heart conditions and fluid retention. In herbal preparations, the leaves of Digitalis × coutinhoi are often used to create tinctures, infusions, and ointments, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of cardiovascular issues, although caution and professional guidance are advised due to the plant's toxicity.
